skill.name: review-code
skill.description: Guides structured code reviews with a senior-engineer checklist for quality, security, performance, maintainability, testing, and follow-up.
skill.summary: Run structured code reviews with a practical senior-engineer checklist.
skill.author: 0xmsc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and external command patterns. The weak cryptography alerts and one Markdown filename backtick alert are false positives. The remaining command guidance is legitimate for code review, but it can run untrusted project scripts during reviews.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run project-specific linting, testing, static analysis, and git commands. This is normal for code review, but commands such as npm test or just test can execute scripts from an untrusted repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly present in the documented workflow. The risk depends on the reviewed project, so this is a legitimate medium-risk capability rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak-cryptography alerts match ordinary prose and section titles, including description, Design, and Design patterns. No cryptographic algorithm use, key handling, or hashing implementation is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language review guidance, not crypto operations. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithms being executed or recommended.

low_findings.title: Markdown Filename Backtick Alert Is False Positive
low_findings.description: A static external-command alert points to a backticked reference filename. This line documents checklist.md and does not instruct command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a Markdown reference entry for a documentation file. It contains no shell command, script invocation, or user-controlled execution path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Untrusted Project Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow recommends running commands from the target project, including lint, test, audit, safety, bandit, semgrep, and git diff. Reviewers should inspect scripts before running them in untrusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly listed as review tools. They are common and useful, but some can invoke repository-controlled scripts.

content.user_title: Review Code With a Senior Checklist
content.value_statement: Code reviews often miss security, performance, testing, and maintainability issues under time pressure. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow and checklist for thorough review feedback.
actual_capabilities: Provides a step-by-step workflow for understanding review scope, executing checks, documenting feedback, and verifying fixes.
Supplies checklist coverage for code quality, security, performance, error handling, maintainability, testing, version control, and infrastructure.
Suggests common local checks such as linting, test runners, security scanners, and git diff review.
Helps structure review comments with rationale, priority, and actionable suggestions.
Supports review planning for pull requests, code changes, and follow-up verification.
limitations: It does not run tools by itself unless the host AI agent has tool access and permission.
It does not replace project-specific architecture knowledge or maintainer judgment.
It provides a general checklist, not language-specific static analysis rules.
Running project commands can be risky when the repository is untrusted.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review a feature or bug-fix pull request with consistent checks for correctness, tests, security, and maintainability.

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Change Review
use_cases.description: Check authentication, authorization, input validation, secrets handling, dependency risk, and error disclosure before approval.

use_cases.title: Review Process Coaching
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable review routine for teams that need clearer feedback and fewer missed issues.

prompt_templates.title: Review This Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the review-code skill to review this change. Focus on correctness, tests, security, and maintainability. Give prioritized findings with file and line references when available.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a review before reading the full diff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the review-code workflow to plan a pull request review. Identify the likely risk areas, useful commands to consider, and the checklist sections that matter most.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Risky Diff
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a security-sensitive or infrastructure change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the review-code checklist to audit this diff for security, reliability, performance, and rollback risk. Separate blocking findings from advisory comments.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Review Fixes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced follow-up after the author updates the code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the review-code follow-up step to verify whether the latest changes address previous findings. Note unresolved issues, regressions, and tests that should be rerun.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes authentication checks and adds new tests.
output: Findings are grouped by severity with concise rationale.
Security comments cover authorization bypass risk and sensitive error messages.
Testing feedback identifies missing negative cases and follow-up verification steps.

output_examples.input: Plan a review for a database migration and API update.
output: The review plan highlights transaction safety, rollback, indexes, API compatibility, and monitoring.
Suggested checks include migration tests, integration tests, and a git diff pass before approval.

output_examples.input: Verify fixes after a reviewer requested changes.
output: The response maps each prior finding to its current status.
Remaining risks are listed separately from resolved items and optional improvements.

best_practices: Inspect project scripts before running lint, test, or audit commands in an untrusted repository.
Ask for the ticket, pull request description, and relevant design context before judging the diff.
Prioritize findings by user impact, security impact, correctness, and maintainability.
anti_patterns: Do not approve a change based only on style checks when behavior and tests are unclear.
Do not run repository-controlled commands blindly in untrusted codebases.
Do not give vague review comments without rationale or a concrete suggested fix.

faq.question: What does this skill help review?
faq.answer: It helps review code changes, pull requests, tests, security concerns, performance risks, and maintainability issues.

faq.question: Can it run tests or linters automatically?
faq.answer: Only if the host AI tool has command access. Reviewers should inspect scripts before running commands from untrusted repositories.

faq.question: Is this skill language-specific?
faq.answer: No. It provides a general review workflow and checklist that can be applied across many programming languages.

faq.question: Does it replace human code review?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure analysis and feedback, but maintainers still need to judge product context and tradeoffs.

faq.question: How should findings be reported?
faq.answer: Findings should include severity, evidence, rationale, and a practical suggestion when a fix is clear.

faq.question: Why is the security risk rated medium?
faq.answer: The skill recommends running local project commands. That is useful for review, but untrusted scripts can execute code.
